title: DevHarness Architecture Draft Output
description: Output contract for the DevHarness architecture draft gate before critic attack.

outcome.description: Machine-readable stage outcome used by workflow routing.
outcome.x-usage: Downstream next/match logic reads this value; choose only the enum/const value that matches the stage state.

decision.description: Architecture decision or routing conclusion for the scoped change.
decision.x-usage: State the selected architecture approach, including whether artifacts are required or deferred.

entities.description: Architecture entities, ownership zones, or contract surfaces affected by the task.
entities.x-usage: Use for structural handoff; keep implementation details out unless needed to define boundaries.

boundaries.description: Boundaries, seams, dependencies, or ownership rules the implementation must respect.
boundaries.x-usage: Downstream planning and review use these to catch scope creep and dependency violations.

non_goals.description: Explicitly excluded work for this architecture slice.
non_goals.x-usage: Use to prevent planner and implementers from widening the task.

architecture_contract.description: Architecture draft contract covering decisions, entities, boundaries, and non-goals for the scoped implementation.
architecture_contract.x-usage: Planner and attack steps consume this as the authoritative architecture boundary and entity record.

artifacts.description: Optional structured artifacts emitted by this stage.
artifacts.x-usage: Use for named files or durable handoff artifacts the orchestrator should preserve.

results.description: Optional structured result summaries emitted by this stage.
results.x-usage: Use for compact machine-readable evidence or decisions that should remain in baton state.

summary.description: Concise human-readable summary for this contract section.

source_step_id.description: Workflow step id that discovered or owns the blocker.

needed.description: Specific missing input, decision, permission, or evidence needed to proceed.

evidence.description: Evidence references supporting the handoff, verdict, or blocker.

risk.description: Residual risk if the blocker or finding is ignored.

blocker.description: Blocker details required when the stage cannot produce a usable contract.
blocker.x-usage: Populate when outcome is blocked so the orchestrator can stop and ask for the missing input or decision.
